message.content:
use  ultrathink - Navegue por toda a documentacao do bmad atualizada e então execute a seguinte tarefa/responda: O bmad está configurado conforme
instrucoes atualizadas no repositorio do bmad (https://github.com/bmad-code-org/BMAD-METHOD)? 
---  remova todas as pastas da raiz após recriar todo o bmad usando as instrucoes recomendadas atualizadas - sua referencia são os
arquivos na pasta docs, os arquivos atuais do bmad não devem ser usados parcial ou totalmente sem antes avaliar o conteúdo deles de forma
minuciosa e também holistica em relacao aos objetivos do projeto e a ultima versão do bmad method (alpha) descritas no github. 

O bmad atual foi criado pelo claude code (você) mas nunca usado e por ser LLM provavelmente contenha erros/alucinações, nem sabemos se funciona, se está correto, etc... então não vamos migrar o bmad, vamos recriar com base nos documentos em ./docs e aprendendo/avaliando antes de usar qualquer coisa do bmad atual. Responda de forma sucinta o que você entendeu antes de começar e aguarde 'OK'

content.text:
## Entendi o seguinte:

1. **Objetivo**: Recriar o BMAD do zero, usando a versão alpha atualizada do repositório oficial (github.com/bmad-code-org/BMAD-METHOD)

2. **Fontes confiáveis**:
   - Documentação oficial do GitHub (versão alpha atualizada)
   - Arquivos em `./docs` do projeto atual

3. **O que NÃO usar diretamente**:
   - Pasta `bmad-knowledge/` atual (pode conter alucinações/erros do Claude)
   - Qualquer arquivo BMAD existente sem avaliação minuciosa

4. **Processo**:
   - Primeiro: estudar a documentação oficial do BMAD no GitHub
   - Segundo: avaliar os docs do projeto (`./docs`) para entender objetivos
   - Terceiro: recriar o BMAD corretamente
   - Quarto: remover pastas antigas da raiz

5. **Premissa**: O BMAD atual nunca foi validado/usado, foi gerado por LLM e pode estar incorreto - então é uma recriação, não migração.

Aguardo **OK** para iniciar a análise da documentação oficial do BMAD Method.

content.content:
# BMAD Method: Comprehensive Overview

## What is BMAD Method?

BMAD Method is an AI-driven agile development framework built on BMad Core (Collaboration Optimized Reflection Engine). It provides "structured, battle-tested workflows powered by specialized agents who understand agile development." The system includes 21 specialized AI agents across 4 official modules and 50+ guided workflows that scale from bug fixes to enterprise platforms.

## Core Components

### Three Main Modules:
1. **BMad Method (BMM)** - Complete agile development with 12 specialized agents and 34 workflows across 4 phases
2. **BMad Builder (BMB)** - Tool for creating custom agents and domain-specific solutions
3. **Creative Intelligence Suite (CIS)** - Innovation workflows including brainstorming and design thinking

### Development Phases:
1. Analysis (optional) - Brainstorming and research
2. Planning - PRDs and technical specifications
3. Solutioning - Architecture and UX design
4. Implementation - Story-driven development

## Installation Instructions

```bash
# Install v6 Alpha (recommended)
npx bmad-method@alpha install

# Or stable v4 for production
npx bmad-method install
```

## Setup Process

After installation, initialize your project in any IDE:
```
*workflow-init
```

This command analyzes your project and recommends the appropriate workflow track.

## Three Workflow Tracks

| Track | Use Case | Planning Scope | Duration |
|-------|----------|-----------------|----------|
| Quick Flow | Bug fixes, small features | Tech spec only | <5 minutes |
| BMad Method | Products, platforms | PRD + Architecture + UX | <15 minutes |
| Enterprise | Compliance, scale | Full governance suite | <30 minutes |

## Key Features

- **Scale-Adaptive Intelligence** - Adjusts planning depth automatically
- **Customizable Agents** - Modify personalities and expertise
- **Multi-Language Support** - Separate settings for communication and code
- **Document Sharding** - "90% token savings for large projects"
- **Update-Safe Customization** - Configurations persist through updates
- **Web Bundles** - Compatible with ChatGPT, Claude Projects, Gemini Gems

## Repository Structure

```
.github/              - Configuration and workflows
.husky/               - Git hooks
.vscode/              - VS Code settings
docs/                 - Documentation files
samples/sample-custom-modules/  - Example custom modules
src/                  - Source code for modules
test/                 - Test files
tools/                - Development utilities
website/              - Web documentation
```

## Essential Files

- **README.md** - Main project documentation
- **CONTRIBUTING.md** - Development guidelines
- **LICENSE** - MIT License
- **package.json** - Dependencies and scripts
- **eslint.config.mjs** - Code linting configuration
- **.coderabbit.yaml** - Code review settings

## Critical Documentation Links

- [Quick Start Guide](https://github.com/bmad-code-org/BMAD-METHOD/blob/main/src/modules/bmm/docs/quick-start.md)
- [Complete BMM Documentation](https://github.com/bmad-code-org/BMAD-METHOD/blob/main/src/modules/bmm/docs/index.md)
- [Agent Customization Guide](https://github.com/bmad-code-org/BMAD-METHOD/blob/main/docs/agent-customization-guide.md)
- [Custom Content Installation](https://github.com/bmad-code-org/BMAD-METHOD/blob/main/docs/custom-content-installation.md)
- [v4 to v6 Upgrade Guide](https://github.com/bmad-code-org/BMAD-METHOD/blob/main/docs/v4-to-v6-upgrade.md)

## Specialized Agents

The system includes 12 core agents: Developer, Architect, PM, Scrum Master, UX Designer, Test Architect, Analyst, BMad Master, Tech Writer, Game Architect, Game Designer, and Game Developer.

## Development Commands

```bash
npm test              # Run quality checks
npm run lint:fix      # Fix code style
npm run format:fix    # Auto-format code
npm run bundle        # Build web bundles
```

## Community Resources

- **Discord**: Community support and collaboration
- **GitHub Issues**: Bug reports and feature requests
- **YouTube Channel**: Video tutorials and demonstrations
- **Web Bundles**: Pre-built agent packages

## Version Information

- **Current Stable**: v4.44.1
- **Current Alpha**: v6 (near-beta quality)
- **License**: MIT
- **Required Node.js**: ≥20.0.0
- **Repository**: 1,108 commits, 26.3k stars, 3.7k forks

## New in v6

Complete architectural overhaul featuring modular design, scale-adaptive planning, visual workflow diagrams, custom module creation capabilities, and expanded agent specialization with personality customization.

content.content:
# BMAD Method V6 Quick Start: Installation & Initialization

## Installation Steps

The installation process is straightforward:

```bash
npx bmad-method@alpha install
```

According to the guide, "The interactive installer will guide you through setup and create a `_bmad/` folder with all agents and workflows."

## Folder Structure Created

The installation generates a `_bmad/` directory containing all necessary agents and workflow files for your project.

## Files Generated During Initialization

Two primary tracking files are created as you progress:

1. **bmm-workflow-status.yaml** - Created by the workflow-init process in your project's docs folder (default location). This file monitors progress through all phases and indicates which workflows are optional, recommended, or required next.

2. **sprint-status.yaml** - Generated during Phase 4 (Implementation) by the sprint-planning workflow. This tracks epics and stories throughout the building phase.

## Project Initialization Process

The initialization workflow (workflow-init) is launched by loading the Analyst agent and running the workflow. During this process, you'll describe:

- Your project goals and objectives
- Whether the project is entirely new or uses existing code
- General scope and complexity level

## Planning Track Selection

Based on your project description, the system suggests and allows you to choose from three tracks:

- **Quick Flow** - Minimal planning for bug fixes/simple features (1-15 stories typically)
- **BMad Method** - Full planning with PRD and architecture (10-50+ stories typically)
- **Enterprise Method** - Extended planning including security/DevOps considerations (30+ stories typically)

The guide emphasizes: "Story counts are guidance, not definitions. Tracks are chosen based on planning needs, not story math."

content.content:
# BMAD Quick Start: Installation & Project Initialization

## Installation

The setup process is straightforward:

```bash
npx bmad-method@alpha install
```

The interactive installer guides users through setup and creates a `_bmad/` folder containing all agents and workflows.

## Project Initialization Workflow

After installation, users load the Analyst agent and execute the "workflow-init" workflow. This interactive process gathers information about:

- Project goals and description
- Whether the codebase is new or existing
- General size and complexity estimates

## Planning Track Selection

Based on the provided information, the system suggests and allows users to choose from three tracks:

1. **Quick Flow** – "Fast implementation (tech-spec only) - bug fixes, simple features, clear scope"
2. **BMad Method** – "Full planning (PRD + Architecture + UX) - products, platforms, complex features"
3. **Enterprise Method** – "Extended planning (BMad Method + Security/DevOps/Test) - enterprise requirements"

## Generated Artifacts

Upon confirmation, the system creates `bmm-workflow-status.yaml` in the project's docs folder. This file tracks progress through all phases and updates automatically as work progresses.

## Folder Structure

The installation establishes a `_bmad/` directory housing agents and workflows organized by function—no manual folder creation is required.

1→# MMS - Metro Mileage Management System
     2→
     3→> Enterprise solution to centralize and automate mileage management for mixed rolling stock fleets (>100 units), bridging Operations and Maintenance via predictive, data-driven planning integrated with EAM.
     4→
     5→---
     6→
     7→## Overview
     8→
     9→MMS replaces manual workflows with a rule-based, predictive engine that:
    10→- Optimizes preventative maintenance scheduling

    13→
    14→---
    15→
    16→## Domain Glossary

    22→| **Maintenance Profile** | Set of PM intervals, overhaul schedules, and resource requirements specific to a fleet type                                    | Entity      |

    24→| **Train Fitness**       | Binary operational readiness (fit/unfit) determined by Maintenance team or EAM. Unfit trains are excluded from run assignments | State       |
    25→| **Run Assignment**      | Daily allocation of train IDs to routes/network based on fitness and mileage targets                                           | Action      |
    26→| **Mileage Balancing**   | Strategy to equalize wear across fleet, preventing underrun/overrun                                                            | Goal        |
    27→| **EAM**                 | Enterprise Asset Management - external system for asset lifecycle (e.g., IBM Maximo, SAP PM)                                   | Integration |
    28→| **TCO**                 | Total Cost of Ownership over fleet lifecycle                                                                                   | Metric      |
    29→
    30→---
    31→
    32→## System Inputs
    33→
    34→| Category        | Input                     | Description                                |
    35→|-----------------|---------------------------|--------------------------------------------|
    36→| **Fleet**       | Train IDs                 | Unique identifier per train                |
    37→|                 | Fleet Type                | Classification/manufacturer grouping       |
    38→|                 | Base Depot                | Designated maintenance depot per fleet     |
    39→| **Mileage**     | Daily Mileage             | Real-time km per train (live data)         |
    40→| **Operations**  | Train Fitness Status      | Daily fitness state per train              |
    41→|                 | Operation Requirements    | Daily demand per train run ID              |
    42→| **Maintenance** | Maintenance Plans         | PM schedules, overhaul intervals           |
    43→|                 | Track Availability        | Maintenance track slots                    |
    44→|                 | Man-hours per Maintenance | Labor hours by maintenance type            |
    45→| **Costs**       | Material Cost             | Spare parts cost per maintenance type      |
    46→|                 | Service Cost              | External service cost per maintenance type |
    47→|                 | Man-hour Cost             | Labor rate                                 |
    48→
    49→---
    50→
    51→## System Outputs
    52→
    53→| Output                   | Description                                                                | Format         |
    54→|--------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------|

    56→| **Maintenance Status**   | Current mileage position per train relative to next PM/overhaul            | Web UI         |
    57→| **Run Assignments**      | Daily train allocation recommendations based on fitness + mileage balance  | Web UI, Export |
    58→| **Maintenance Schedule** | Completed and upcoming PM/overhaul activities                              | Web UI, Export |

    60→| **Cost Compliance**      | Actual vs. Planned maintenance costs (monthly/yearly)                      | Web UI, Export |
    61→| **Budget Forecast**      | Projected maintenance costs per fleet over planning horizon                | Web UI, Export |
    62→| **Simulation Reports**   | Scenario comparison: cost impact, compliance risk, fleet wear distribution | PDF, Excel     |
    63→| **Standard Reports**     | Configurable reports for stakeholders                                      | PDF, Excel     |
    64→| **Notifications**        | Alerts via in-app and email                                                | Push, Email    |
    65→
    66→---
    67→
    68→## Core Modules
    69→
    70→### 1. Maintenance Planning
    71→
    72→Generates optimized schedules from short-term to long-term horizons.
    73→
    74→| Horizon    | Scope                     | Objective                              |
    75→|------------|---------------------------|----------------------------------------|
    76→| Short-term | Weekly, monthly schedules | Execution feasibility, resource lock   |
    77→| Long-term  | 1-10 year strategic plans | Budget forecasting, lifecycle strategy |
    78→
    79→**Constraints:**
    80→- Man-hour availability
    81→- Track allocation
    82→- Train availability
    83→
    84→**Outputs:**
    85→- Optimized maintenance schedules
    86→- Warnings for mileage underrun/overrun risks
    87→- Scenario simulation capabilities
    88→
    89→---
    90→
    91→### 2. Operation Planning
    92→
    93→Dynamic assignment engine for daily fleet deployment.
    94→
    95→**Flow:**
    96→1. **Input:** Daily operation requirements (train run IDs, network demand)
    97→2. **Process:** Filters available trains by fitness → Ranks by mileage delta (target vs actual)
    98→3. **Output:** `TrainID` + network/route assignment recommendation
    99→4. **Loop:** Ingests actual daily km to update predictions and trigger corrections
   100→
   101→**Interface:** Unified Maintenance ↔ Ops communication for train fitness and mileage management.
   102→
   103→---
   104→
   105→### 3. Resource Planning
   106→
   107→Strategic module for maintenance costing and resource optimization.
   108→
   109→**Capabilities:**
   110→- Resource smoothing: distributes man-hours and spare parts evenly
   111→- Prevents resource peaks/troughs over planning horizon
   112→- Maintenance costing per fleet based on mileage and plan
   113→
   114→**Fixed Parameters:**

   123→| Phase       | Scope                                                                                 | Status |
   124→|-------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------|

   126→| **Phase 2** | Financial modeling (costing per fleet, budget compliance), EAM integration capability | -      |
   127→
   128→---
   129→
   130→## Architecture & Data Flow
   131→
   132→```
   133→[Operations] <--(Interface/Comms)--> [Maintenance]
   134→      |                                    |
   135→      | (Actuals)                          | (Plans)
   136→      v                                    v
   137→+-----------------------------------------------------+
   138→|                        MMS                          |
   139→|  +-----------+-----------+-----------+              |
   140→|  |Maintenance| Operation | Resource  |              |
   141→|  | Planning  | Planning  | Planning  |              |
   142→|  +-----------+-----------+-----------+              |
   143→+--------------------+--------------------------------+
   144→                     | (Work Orders / Asset Data)

   146→              +-------------+

   151→

   153→
   154→## User Roles
   155→

   157→|-----------------|----------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------|
   158→| **Admin**       | System-wide                      | Full configuration, user management, all data access             | None                                            |
   159→| **Maintenance** | Planning, scheduling, resources  | Edit maintenance plans, track allocation, cost data, set fitness | Cannot edit run assignments or mileage actuals  |
   160→| **Operations**  | Run assignments, daily execution | Edit daily assignments, input mileage actuals                    | Cannot edit maintenance plans or fitness status |
   161→
   162→---
   163→
   164→## For AI Agents
   165→
   166→**System purpose:** Optimize rolling stock maintenance scheduling and mileage distribution.
   167→
   168→**Optimization objective:** Minimize mileage variance across fleet while respecting maintenance windows and resource constraints.
   169→
   170→### Entity Model
   171→
   172→```
   173→Fleet (1) ---- (*) Train
   174→  |                 |
   175→  |                 +---- (*) RunAssignment
   176→  |                 |
   177→  |                 +---- (1) TrainFitness [daily state, set by Maintenance]
   178→  |
   179→  +---- (1) BaseDepot
   180→  |
   181→  +---- (1) MaintenanceProfile
   182→
   183→MaintenancePlan (1) ---- (*) MaintenanceActivity
   184→       |
   185→       +---- constrains ---- RunAssignment
   186→
   187→Cost
   188→  +-- MaterialCost [per maintenance type]
   189→  +-- ServiceCost [per maintenance type]
   190→  +-- ManHourCost [rate]
   191→```
   192→
   193→### Optional Integrations
   194→
   195→```
   196→EAM (external)
   197→  +-- syncs ---- AssetData [master]
   198→  +-- syncs ---- WorkOrders
   199→```
   200→
   201→### Fixed Parameters (Constraints)
   202→
   203→- Man-hour limits (per depot, per period)
   204→- Track availability (slots per depot)
   205→- Cost baselines (material, service, labor)
   206→
   207→### Configurable Thresholds
   208→
   209→| Parameter                | Default | Description                  |
   210→|--------------------------|---------|------------------------------|
   211→| `mileage_underrun_pct`   | -10%    | Trigger warning below target |
   212→| `mileage_overrun_pct`    | +10%    | Trigger warning above target |
   213→| `planning_horizon_years` | 10      | Long-term planning window    |
   214→
   215→### Business Rules
   216→
   217→- `IF` predicted ≠ actual mileage `THEN` trigger warning + recommend adjustment
   218→- `IF` train approaching underrun/overrun threshold `THEN` trigger warning + corrective guidance
   219→- `IF` train unfit `THEN` exclude from run assignment candidates
   220→- Resource planning must avoid peaks/troughs over planning horizon
   221→
   222→### Constraint Priority (highest to lowest)
   223→
   224→1. **Train Fitness** - Unfit trains never assigned
   225→2. **Maintenance Windows** - Scheduled maintenance blocks availability
   226→3. **Resource Limits** - Man-hours, track slots per depot
   227→4. **Mileage Balance** - Optimize within above constraints
   228→
   229→### Integration Points
   230→
   231→- **Input:** Daily mileage (live), fitness status, operation requirements
   232→- **Output:** Optimized schedules, run assignments, compliance reports
   233→- **External (optional):** EAM sync - work orders, asset master data
   234→
   235→---
   236→
   237→## Platform Capabilities
   238→
   239→**Interface:**
   240→- Web-based application (desktop + mobile)
   241→- Centralized dashboard with fleet-wide visibility
   242→- Customizable alerts/notifications (in-app + email)
   243→
   244→**Technical:**
   245→- Scalable architecture for additional fleets
   246→- Data security and IT compliance
   247→- EAM integration capability (API-based)
   248→
   249→**Reports:**
   250→- Standard and custom report generation (PDF, Excel)
   251→- Simulation reports for different mileage management strategies
   252→- Email notification of generated reports to stakeholders

file.content:
# MMS - Metro Mileage Management System

> Enterprise solution to centralize and automate mileage management for mixed rolling stock fleets (>100 units), bridging Operations and Maintenance via predictive, data-driven planning integrated with EAM.

---

## Overview

MMS replaces manual workflows with a rule-based, predictive engine that:
- Optimizes preventative maintenance scheduling
- Balances mileage across the fleet
- Provides unified interface between Maintenance and Operations teams

---

## Domain Glossary

| Term                    | Definition                                                                                                                     | Type        |
|-------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------|
| **Rolling Stock**       | Railway vehicles (trains, carriages) requiring maintenance                                                                     | Entity      |
| **Fleet**               | Group of rolling stock sharing same manufacturer/model and maintenance profile                                                 | Entity      |
| **Maintenance Profile** | Set of PM intervals, overhaul schedules, and resource requirements specific to a fleet type                                    | Entity      |
| **Base Depot**          | Designated maintenance facility for a fleet                                                                                    | Entity      |
| **Train Fitness**       | Binary operational readiness (fit/unfit) determined by Maintenance team or EAM. Unfit trains are excluded from run assignments | State       |
| **Run Assignment**      | Daily allocation of train IDs to routes/network based on fitness and mileage targets                                           | Action      |
| **Mileage Balancing**   | Strategy to equalize wear across fleet, preventing underrun/overrun                                                            | Goal        |
| **EAM**                 | Enterprise Asset Management - external system for asset lifecycle (e.g., IBM Maximo, SAP PM)                                   | Integration |
| **TCO**                 | Total Cost of Ownership over fleet lifecycle                                                                                   | Metric      |

---

## System Inputs

| Category        | Input                     | Description                                |
|-----------------|---------------------------|--------------------------------------------|
| **Fleet**       | Train IDs                 | Unique identifier per train                |
|                 | Fleet Type                | Classification/manufacturer grouping       |
|                 | Base Depot                | Designated maintenance depot per fleet     |
| **Mileage**     | Daily Mileage             | Real-time km per train (live data)         |
| **Operations**  | Train Fitness Status      | Daily fitness state per train              |
|                 | Operation Requirements    | Daily demand per train run ID              |
| **Maintenance** | Maintenance Plans         | PM schedules, overhaul intervals           |
|                 | Track Availability        | Maintenance track slots                    |
|                 | Man-hours per Maintenance | Labor hours by maintenance type            |
| **Costs**       | Material Cost             | Spare parts cost per maintenance type      |
|                 | Service Cost              | External service cost per maintenance type |
|                 | Man-hour Cost             | Labor rate                                 |

---

## System Outputs

| Output                   | Description                                                                | Format         |
|--------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------|
| **Dashboard**            | Fleet-wide mileage status, high-level KPIs                                 | Web UI         |
| **Maintenance Status**   | Current mileage position per train relative to next PM/overhaul            | Web UI         |
| **Run Assignments**      | Daily train allocation recommendations based on fitness + mileage balance  | Web UI, Export |
| **Maintenance Schedule** | Completed and upcoming PM/overhaul activities                              | Web UI, Export |
| **Compliance Status**    | Real-time adherence to maintenance requirements                            | Web UI         |
| **Cost Compliance**      | Actual vs. Planned maintenance costs (monthly/yearly)                      | Web UI, Export |
| **Budget Forecast**      | Projected maintenance costs per fleet over planning horizon                | Web UI, Export |
| **Simulation Reports**   | Scenario comparison: cost impact, compliance risk, fleet wear distribution | PDF, Excel     |
| **Standard Reports**     | Configurable reports for stakeholders                                      | PDF, Excel     |
| **Notifications**        | Alerts via in-app and email                                                | Push, Email    |

---

## Core Modules

### 1. Maintenance Planning

Generates optimized schedules from short-term to long-term horizons.

| Horizon    | Scope                     | Objective                              |
|------------|---------------------------|----------------------------------------|
| Short-term | Weekly, monthly schedules | Execution feasibility, resource lock   |
| Long-term  | 1-10 year strategic plans | Budget forecasting, lifecycle strategy |

**Constraints:**
- Man-hour availability
- Track allocation
- Train availability

**Outputs:**
- Optimized maintenance schedules
- Warnings for mileage underrun/overrun risks
- Scenario simulation capabilities

---

### 2. Operation Planning

Dynamic assignment engine for daily fleet deployment.

**Flow:**
1. **Input:** Daily operation requirements (train run IDs, network demand)
2. **Process:** Filters available trains by fitness → Ranks by mileage delta (target vs actual)
3. **Output:** `TrainID` + network/route assignment recommendation
4. **Loop:** Ingests actual daily km to update predictions and trigger corrections

**Interface:** Unified Maintenance ↔ Ops communication for train fitness and mileage management.

---

### 3. Resource Planning

Strategic module for maintenance costing and resource optimization.

**Capabilities:**
- Resource smoothing: distributes man-hours and spare parts evenly
- Prevents resource peaks/troughs over planning horizon
- Maintenance costing per fleet based on mileage and plan

**Fixed Parameters:**
- Manpower availability
- Spare parts costs (material + service)
- Track capacity

---

## Delivery Phases

| Phase       | Scope                                                                                 | Status |
|-------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------|
| **Phase 1** | Mileage management, run assignments                                                   | -      |
| **Phase 2** | Financial modeling (costing per fleet, budget compliance), EAM integration capability | -      |

---

## Architecture & Data Flow

```
[Operations] <--(Interface/Comms)--> [Maintenance]
      |                                    |
      | (Actuals)                          | (Plans)
      v                                    v
+-----------------------------------------------------+
|                        MMS                          |
|  +-----------+-----------+-----------+              |
|  |Maintenance| Operation | Resource  |              |
|  | Planning  | Planning  | Planning  |              |
|  +-----------+-----------+-----------+              |
+--------------------+--------------------------------+
                     | (Work Orders / Asset Data)
                     v
              +-------------+
              |     EAM     |
              |(Integration)|
              +-------------+
```

---

## User Roles

| Role            | Scope                            | Permissions                                                      | Restrictions                                    |
|-----------------|----------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------|
| **Admin**       | System-wide                      | Full configuration, user management, all data access             | None                                            |
| **Maintenance** | Planning, scheduling, resources  | Edit maintenance plans, track allocation, cost data, set fitness | Cannot edit run assignments or mileage actuals  |
| **Operations**  | Run assignments, daily execution | Edit daily assignments, input mileage actuals                    | Cannot edit maintenance plans or fitness status |

---

## For AI Agents

**System purpose:** Optimize rolling stock maintenance scheduling and mileage distribution.

**Optimization objective:** Minimize mileage variance across fleet while respecting maintenance windows and resource constraints.

### Entity Model

```
Fleet (1) ---- (*) Train
  |                 |
  |                 +---- (*) RunAssignment
  |                 |
  |                 +---- (1) TrainFitness [daily state, set by Maintenance]
  |
  +---- (1) BaseDepot
  |
  +---- (1) MaintenanceProfile

MaintenancePlan (1) ---- (*) MaintenanceActivity
       |
       +---- constrains ---- RunAssignment

Cost
  +-- MaterialCost [per maintenance type]
  +-- ServiceCost [per maintenance type]
  +-- ManHourCost [rate]
```

### Optional Integrations

```
EAM (external)
  +-- syncs ---- AssetData [master]
  +-- syncs ---- WorkOrders
```

### Fixed Parameters (Constraints)

- Man-hour limits (per depot, per period)
- Track availability (slots per depot)
- Cost baselines (material, service, labor)

### Configurable Thresholds

| Parameter                | Default | Description                  |
|--------------------------|---------|------------------------------|
| `mileage_underrun_pct`   | -10%    | Trigger warning below target |
| `mileage_overrun_pct`    | +10%    | Trigger warning above target |
| `planning_horizon_years` | 10      | Long-term planning window    |

### Business Rules

- `IF` predicted ≠ actual mileage `THEN` trigger warning + recommend adjustment
- `IF` train approaching underrun/overrun threshold `THEN` trigger warning + corrective guidance
- `IF` train unfit `THEN` exclude from run assignment candidates
- Resource planning must avoid peaks/troughs over planning horizon

### Constraint Priority (highest to lowest)

1. **Train Fitness** - Unfit trains never assigned
2. **Maintenance Windows** - Scheduled maintenance blocks availability
3. **Resource Limits** - Man-hours, track slots per depot
4. **Mileage Balance** - Optimize within above constraints

### Integration Points

- **Input:** Daily mileage (live), fitness status, operation requirements
- **Output:** Optimized schedules, run assignments, compliance reports
- **External (optional):** EAM sync - work orders, asset master data

---

## Platform Capabilities

**Interface:**
- Web-based application (desktop + mobile)
- Centralized dashboard with fleet-wide visibility
- Customizable alerts/notifications (in-app + email)

**Technical:**
- Scalable architecture for additional fleets
- Data security and IT compliance
- EAM integration capability (API-based)

**Reports:**
- Standard and custom report generation (PDF, Excel)
- Simulation reports for different mileage management strategies
- Email notification of generated reports to stakeholders

1→# BMAD Method Implementation Plan for MMS Project
     2→
     3→> **Comprehensive plan to implement the BMAD (Build More, Architect Dreams) methodology for the Metro Mileage Management System demo project.**
     4→>
     5→> **Target:** Win railway fleet management system bid in 3 months
     6→> **Method:** BMAD V6 with customized domain-specific agents
     7→
     8→---
     9→
    10→## Executive Summary
    11→
    12→This plan integrates the BMAD Method V6 framework with the MMS Demo Playbook to create a structured, AI-driven development approach. The BMAD method provides:
    13→
    14→- **21 specialized AI agents** for different roles (PM, Architect, Developer, UX, etc.)
    15→- **4-phase workflow** (Analysis → Planning → Solutioning → Implementation)
    16→- **Scale-adaptive planning** automatically adjusting to project complexity
    17→- **Story-centric implementation** with sprint management
    18→
    19→Given the MMS project's scope (demo with tight timeline), we'll use the **BMad Method Track** (Level 2-3) with customizations for the railway domain.
    20→
    21→---
    22→
    23→## Table of Contents
    24→
    25→1. [BMAD Method Overview](#1-bmad-method-overview)
    26→2. [MMS Project Mapping](#2-mms-project-mapping)
    27→3. [Implementation Structure](#3-implementation-structure)
    28→4. [Phase-by-Phase Plan](#4-phase-by-phase-plan)
    29→5. [Customized Agents for MMS](#5-customized-agents-for-mms)
    30→6. [Workflow Sequence](#6-workflow-sequence)
    31→7. [Sprint Plan Integration](#7-sprint-plan-integration)
    32→8. [Folder Structure](#8-folder-structure)
    33→9. [Installation Steps](#9-installation-steps)
    34→10. [Success Metrics](#10-success-metrics)
    35→
    36→---
    37→
    38→## 1. BMAD Method Overview
    39→
    40→### 1.1 What is BMAD?
    41→
    42→BMAD (Build More, Architect Dreams) is an AI-driven agile development methodology built on **BMad Core** (Collaboration Optimized Reflection Engine). It provides:
    43→
    44→| Component | Description |
    45→|-----------|-------------|
    46→| **BMad Method (BMM)** | Core agile development framework with 12 agents and 34+ workflows |
    47→| **BMad Builder (BMB)** | Tools to create custom agents, workflows, and modules |
    48→| **Creative Intelligence Suite (CIS)** | Innovation and brainstorming workflows |
    49→
    50→### 1.2 Four-Phase Workflow
    51→
    52→```
    53→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    54→│                          BMAD 4-PHASE WORKFLOW                               │
    55→├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
    56→│                                                                              │
    57→│   ┌──────────────┐    ┌──────────────┐    ┌──────────────┐    ┌───────────┐│
    58→│   │   PHASE 1    │    │   PHASE 2    │    │   PHASE 3    │    │  PHASE 4  ││
    59→│   │   Analysis   │───▶│   Planning   │───▶│  Solutioning │───▶│  Implement││
    60→│   │  (Required)  │    │  (Required)  │    │  (Required)  │    │ (Iterative││
    61→│   └──────────────┘    └──────────────┘    └──────────────┘    └───────────┘│
    62→│         │                    │                   │                   │       │
    63→│         ▼                    ▼                   ▼                   ▼       │
    64→│   • Brainstorming      • PRD Creation      • Architecture     • Sprint Plan │
    65→│   • Research           • Tech Specs        • UX/UI Design     • Story Dev   │
    66→│   • Product Brief      • Requirements      • Epic/Stories     • Code Review │
    67→│                                            • Readiness Check  • Testing     │
    68→│                                                                              │
    69→└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    70→```
    71→
    72→### 1.3 Scale-Adaptive Intelligence
    73→
    74→BMAD automatically adjusts to project complexity:
    75→
    76→| Level | Use Case | Planning Depth | MMS Mapping |
    77→|-------|----------|----------------|-------------|
    78→| 0-1 | Bug fixes, small features | Tech spec only | N/A |
    79→| **2** | **Products, platforms** | **PRD + Architecture** | **MMS Demo** |
    80→| 3-4 | Enterprise, compliance | Full governance suite | Future production |
    81→
    82→**MMS fits Level 2-3**: Requires PRD, architecture, and comprehensive UX design.
    83→

   202→## 4. Phase-by-Phase Plan
   203→
   204→### 4.1 Phase 0: BMAD Installation & Configuration
   205→
   206→**Duration:** Day 1-2
   207→**Owner:** Tech Lead
   208→
   209→#### Actions:
   210→
   211→1. **Install BMAD Framework**
   212→   ```bash
   213→   cd /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/mms/MFC
   214→   npx bmad-method@alpha install
   215→   ```
   216→
   217→2. **Configure Project Context**
   218→   - Create `_bmad/project-context.md` with MMS-specific settings
   219→   - Configure language settings (Portuguese for docs, English for code)
   220→   - Set planning_artifacts path to `bmad-artifacts/`
   221→   - Set implementation_artifacts path to `bmad-artifacts/phase-4-implementation/`
   222→
   223→3. **Initialize Workflow Status**
   224→   - Run `*workflow-init` with Analyst agent
   225→   - Select "BMad Method" track (Level 2)
   226→   - Set project_name: "MMS - Metro Mileage Management System"
   227→
   228→4. **Create Custom Agents** (see Section 5)
   229→
   230→#### Deliverables:
   231→- [ ] `_bmad/` folder with all agents installed
   232→- [ ] `_bmad/project-context.md` configured
   233→- [ ] `bmm-workflow-status.yaml` initialized
   234→- [ ] Custom railway domain agent created

   378→
   379→### 4.5 Phase 4: Implementation (Iterative)
   380→
   381→**Duration:** Sprints 3-6 (Weeks 5-12)
   382→**BMAD Workflows:** Sprint Planning, Story Development, Code Review
   383→**Agents:** SM, DEV, TEA, Tech Writer
   384→
   385→#### 4.5.1 Sprint Structure
   386→
   387→Following the 12-week timeline from §10:
   388→
   389→| Sprint | Weeks | Focus | BMAD Workflow |
   390→|--------|-------|-------|---------------|
   391→| 3 | 5-6 | Core Solver + API Foundation | `*sprint-start`, `*work-next-story` |
   392→| 4 | 7-8 | Frontend Base (Dashboard, Map) | `*work-next-story`, `*code-review` |
   393→| 5 | 9-10 | Integration + Validation | `*work-next-story`, `*correct-course` |
   394→| 6 | 11-12 | Demo Polish + Rehearsal | `*sprint-finish`, validation |
   395→
   396→#### 4.5.2 Story Lifecycle
   397→
   398→```
   399→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   400→│                     BMAD STORY LIFECYCLE                            │
   401→├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
   402→│                                                                      │
   403→│   backlog ──▶ ready-for-dev ──▶ in-progress ──▶ review ──▶ done    │
   404→│      │             │                │            │          │       │
   405→│      ▼             ▼                ▼            ▼          ▼       │
   406→│   SM assigns    DEV picks      DEV codes     TEA tests   SM closes │
   407→│   priority      from queue     + unit test   + reviews   sprint    │
   408→│                                                                      │
   409→└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   410→```
   411→
   412→#### 4.5.3 Key Workflows per Sprint
   413→
   414→**Sprint Start:**
   415→```
   416→*sprint-start → SM agent
   417→- Select stories for sprint
   418→- Assign priorities
   419→- Set sprint goals
   420→```
   421→
   422→**Story Development:**
   423→```
   424→*work-next-story → DEV agent
   425→- Get next story from queue
   426→- Receive just-in-time context
   427→- Implement with TDD
   428→```
   429→
   430→**Code Review:**
   431→```
   432→*code-review → TEA agent
   433→- Run automated tests
   434→- Validate against acceptance criteria
   435→- Flag issues for correction
   436→```
   437→
   438→**Sprint End:**
   439→```
   440→*sprint-finish → SM agent
   441→- Update story statuses
   442→- Generate sprint report
   443→- Plan next sprint
   444→```
   445→
   446→#### Deliverables:
   447→- [ ] `bmad-artifacts/phase-4-implementation/sprint-status.yaml` (continuously updated)
   448→- [ ] Sprint folders with completed stories
   449→- [ ] Working demo at end of each sprint
   450→- [ ] Final demo ready by Week 12
   451→

   463→| **UX Designer** | User Experience | Wireframes, component design |

   466→| **TEA** | Test Architect | Testing, quality assurance |

   468→
   469→### 5.2 Custom Agents for MMS Domain
   470→
   471→#### 5.2.1 Railway Domain Expert
   472→
   473→```yaml
   474→# _bmad/custom/agents/railway-domain-expert.agent.yaml
   475→agent:
   476→  metadata:
   477→    id: "_bmad/custom/agents/railway-domain-expert"
   478→    name: Maria
   479→    title: Railway Operations Specialist
   480→    icon: 🚂
   481→    module: custom
   482→
   483→  persona:
   484→    role: Railway Fleet Management Expert
   485→    identity: >
   486→      Senior railway operations professional with 15+ years experience in
   487→      rolling stock management, fleet maintenance, and operational planning.
   488→      Expert in European rail standards (EN 15380, ECM regulations).
   489→    communication_style: >
   490→      "Technical but accessible. Uses precise railway terminology while
   491→      ensuring non-specialists understand. Challenges assumptions with
   492→      real-world operational constraints."
   493→    principles: |
   494→      - Validate all system features against real railway operations
   495→      - Ensure terminology matches industry standards
   496→      - Identify operational constraints that may impact system design
   497→      - Reference MMS_DEMO_PLAYBOOK.md Section 2 for domain context
   498→      - Validate seed data realism against industry norms
   499→
   500→  expertise:
   501→    - Rolling stock lifecycle management
   502→    - Preventive maintenance scheduling (PM1, PM2, HM, Overhaul)
   503→    - Wheelset and bogie maintenance constraints
   504→    - Dead running optimization
   505→    - Depot capacity planning
   506→    - Crew scheduling integration
   507→    - European rail regulations and homologation
   508→
   509→  menu:
   510→    - trigger: VD or validate-domain
   511→      description: "[VD] Validate domain terminology and concepts"
   512→    - trigger: VS or validate-seed
   513→      description: "[VS] Validate seed data realism"
   514→    - trigger: VC or validate-constraints
   515→      description: "[VC] Validate operational constraints in solver"
   516→```
   517→
   518→#### 5.2.2 Demo Coordinator
   519→
   520→```yaml
   521→# _bmad/custom/agents/demo-coordinator.agent.yaml
   522→agent:
   523→  metadata:
   524→    id: "_bmad/custom/agents/demo-coordinator"
   525→    name: Carlos
   526→    title: Demo Presentation Specialist
   527→    icon: 🎯
   528→    module: custom
   529→
   530→  persona:
   531→    role: Enterprise Demo Strategist
   532→    identity: >
   533→      Pre-sales and demo specialist with 10+ years experience in
   534→      enterprise software presentations. Expert in creating "wow moments"
   535→      and handling executive stakeholder objections.
   536→    communication_style: >
   537→      "High-energy, focused on impact. Every feature explained in terms
   538→      of business value. Anticipates objections before they arise."
   539→    principles: |
   540→      - Every demo element must answer "why should the client care?"
   541→      - Reference MMS_DEMO_PLAYBOOK.md Section 12 for demo script
   542→      - Maintain FAQ responses for common questions
   543→      - Ensure 15-minute demo fits timeline perfectly
   544→      - Prepare fallback scenarios for technical issues
   545→
   546→  menu:
   547→    - trigger: DR or demo-review
   548→      description: "[DR] Review demo readiness"
   549→    - trigger: DF or demo-faq
   550→      description: "[DF] Review and expand FAQ"
   551→    - trigger: DS or demo-script
   552→      description: "[DS] Refine demo presentation script"
   553→```

   627→│  ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── │
   628→│                                                                              │

   631→│                                                                              │

   633→│             └── Script refinement, FAQ expansion                             │
   634→│                                                                              │
   635→│  Day 63-65: Demo rehearsals                                                  │
   636→│             └── 3 full runs, bug fixes                                       │
   637→│                                                                              │
   638→│  Day 66:    DEMO DAY                                                         │
   639→│                                                                              │
   640→└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   641→```
   642→
   643→### 6.2 Agent-Workflow Matrix
   644→
   645→| Phase | Primary Agent | Workflows | Output Artifacts |
   646→|-------|---------------|-----------|------------------|

   652→| 3 | PM + SM | `*create-epics-and-stories` | Epics, Stories, Backlog |
   653→| 3 | All | `*check-implementation-readiness` | Readiness checklist |
   654→| 4 | SM | `*sprint-start`, `*sprint-finish` | Sprint plans, reports |
   655→| 4 | DEV | `*work-next-story` | Implemented features |
   656→| 4 | TEA | `*code-review` | Test results, quality reports |
   657→| 4 | Railway Expert | Domain validation | Validated terminology, data |
   658→| 4 | Demo Coordinator | Demo prep | Script, FAQ, fallbacks |

   660→---
   661→
   662→## 7. Sprint Plan Integration
   663→
   664→### 7.1 Mapping Playbook Sprints to BMAD
   665→
   666→The MMS_DEMO_PLAYBOOK.md §10 defines 6 sprints. Here's how they map to BMAD phases:
   667→
   668→| Playbook Sprint | BMAD Phase | BMAD Sprints | Focus |
   669→|-----------------|------------|--------------|-------|
   670→| Sprint 1 (Wk 1-2) | Phase 1+2 | Pre-implementation | Setup, Analysis, PRD |
   671→| Sprint 2 (Wk 3-4) | Phase 2+3 | Pre-implementation | Planning, Architecture |
   672→| Sprint 3 (Wk 5-6) | Phase 4 | BMAD Sprint 1 | Core Solver + API |
   673→| Sprint 4 (Wk 7-8) | Phase 4 | BMAD Sprint 2 | Frontend Base |
   674→| Sprint 5 (Wk 9-10) | Phase 4 | BMAD Sprint 3 | Integration |
   675→| Sprint 6 (Wk 11-12) | Phase 4 | BMAD Sprint 4 | Demo Prep |
   676→
   677→### 7.2 Story Distribution by Sprint
   678→
   679→Based on §5 and §10:
   680→
   681→**BMAD Sprint 1 (Weeks 5-6): Solver + API**
   682→- Stories: 12-15
   683→- Epics: E4 (Allocation core), Backend infrastructure
   684→- Agents: DEV (Python/OR-Tools), Architect
   685→

   687→- Stories: 15-18

   689→- Agents: DEV (Frontend), UX Designer

   692→- Stories: 10-12

   694→- Agents: DEV (Full-stack), TEA, Railway Expert

   697→- Stories: 8-10

   725→
   726→### 8.2 Key Files Description

   739→## 9. Installation Steps

   746→
   747→### 9.2 Step-by-Step Installation
   748→

   775→
   776→### 9.3 Post-Installation Verification

   793→
   794→---

   796→## 10. Success Metrics

   807→
   808→### 10.2 Key Performance Indicators

   818→### 10.3 Risk Mitigation via BMAD

   829→---

   860→
   861→### Common Workflow Commands

# BMAD Method Implementation Plan for MMS Project

> **Comprehensive plan to implement the BMAD (Build More, Architect Dreams) methodology for the Metro Mileage Management System demo project.**
>
> **Target:** Win railway fleet management system bid in 3 months
> **Method:** BMAD V6 with customized domain-specific agents

---

## Executive Summary

This plan integrates the BMAD Method V6 framework with the MMS Demo Playbook to create a structured, AI-driven development approach. The BMAD method provides:

- **21 specialized AI agents** for different roles (PM, Architect, Developer, UX, etc.)
- **4-phase workflow** (Analysis → Planning → Solutioning → Implementation)
- **Scale-adaptive planning** automatically adjusting to project complexity
- **Story-centric implementation** with sprint management

Given the MMS project's scope (demo with tight timeline), we'll use the **BMad Method Track** (Level 2-3) with customizations for the railway domain.

---

## Table of Contents

1. [BMAD Method Overview](#1-bmad-method-overview)
2. [MMS Project Mapping](#2-mms-project-mapping)
3. [Implementation Structure](#3-implementation-structure)
4. [Phase-by-Phase Plan](#4-phase-by-phase-plan)
5. [Customized Agents for MMS](#5-customized-agents-for-mms)
6. [Workflow Sequence](#6-workflow-sequence)
7. [Sprint Plan Integration](#7-sprint-plan-integration)
8. [Folder Structure](#8-folder-structure)
9. [Installation Steps](#9-installation-steps)
10. [Success Metrics](#10-success-metrics)

---

## 1. BMAD Method Overview

### 1.1 What is BMAD?

BMAD (Build More, Architect Dreams) is an AI-driven agile development methodology built on **BMad Core** (Collaboration Optimized Reflection Engine). It provides:

| Component | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| **BMad Method (BMM)** | Core agile development framework with 12 agents and 34+ workflows |
| **BMad Builder (BMB)** | Tools to create custom agents, workflows, and modules |
| **Creative Intelligence Suite (CIS)** | Innovation and brainstorming workflows |

### 1.2 Four-Phase Workflow

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                          BMAD 4-PHASE WORKFLOW                               │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                              │
│   ┌──────────────┐    ┌──────────────┐    ┌──────────────┐    ┌───────────┐│
│   │   PHASE 1    │    │   PHASE 2    │    │   PHASE 3    │    │  PHASE 4  ││
│   │   Analysis   │───▶│   Planning   │───▶│  Solutioning │───▶│  Implement││
│   │  (Required)  │    │  (Required)  │    │  (Required)  │    │ (Iterative││
│   └──────────────┘    └──────────────┘    └──────────────┘    └───────────┘│
│         │                    │                   │                   │       │
│         ▼                    ▼                   ▼                   ▼       │
│   • Brainstorming      • PRD Creation      • Architecture     • Sprint Plan │
│   • Research           • Tech Specs        • UX/UI Design     • Story Dev   │
│   • Product Brief      • Requirements      • Epic/Stories     • Code Review │
│                                            • Readiness Check  • Testing     │
│                                                                              │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### 1.3 Scale-Adaptive Intelligence

BMAD automatically adjusts to project complexity:

| Level | Use Case | Planning Depth | MMS Mapping |
|-------|----------|----------------|-------------|
| 0-1 | Bug fixes, small features | Tech spec only | N/A |
| **2** | **Products, platforms** | **PRD + Architecture** | **MMS Demo** |
| 3-4 | Enterprise, compliance | Full governance suite | Future production |

**MMS fits Level 2-3**: Requires PRD, architecture, and comprehensive UX design.

---

## 2. MMS Project Mapping

### 2.1 Existing Documentation → BMAD Artifacts

The MMS project already has extensive documentation that maps to BMAD artifacts:

| Existing Document | BMAD Artifact | Phase |
|-------------------|---------------|-------|
| `README.md` (Domain overview) | Project Brief | Phase 1 |
| `MMS_DEMO_PLAYBOOK.md` Sections 1-3 | Research/Discovery | Phase 1 |
| `MMS_DEMO_PLAYBOOK.md` Sections 4-5 | PRD (Product Requirements) | Phase 2 |
| `MMS_DEMO_PLAYBOOK.md` Section 6 | Non-Functional Requirements | Phase 2 |
| `MMS_DEMO_PLAYBOOK.md` Section 7 | UX/UI Specification | Phase 3 |
| `MMS_DEMO_PLAYBOOK.md` Section 8 | Architecture Document | Phase 3 |
| `MMS_DEMO_PLAYBOOK.md` Section 9 | Data & Seed Specification | Phase 3 |
| `MMS_DEMO_PLAYBOOK.md` Section 10 | Sprint Plan / Epics | Phase 4 |
| `MMS_PROFESSIONAL_PROFILES.md` | Team Configuration | Setup |

### 2.2 Gap Analysis

| BMAD Requirement | Status | Action Needed |
|------------------|--------|---------------|
| Project Context File | Missing | Create from README.md |
| Formal PRD | Partial (in playbook) | Extract to standalone PRD |
| Architecture Doc | Partial (in playbook) | Formalize with BMAD template |
| Epics & Stories | Missing | Generate from requirements |
| Sprint Status | Missing | Initialize tracking |
| Agent Configurations | Missing | Install and customize |

---

## 3. Implementation Structure

### 3.1 BMAD Folder Structure for MMS

```
/MFC/
├── _bmad/                          # BMAD Core Installation
│   ├── bmm/                        # BMad Method Module
│   │   ├── agents/                 # AI Agent Definitions
│   │   │   ├── analyst.agent.yaml
│   │   │   ├── architect.agent.yaml
│   │   │   ├── dev.agent.yaml
│   │   │   ├── pm.agent.yaml
│   │   │   ├── sm.agent.yaml
│   │   │   ├── tea.agent.yaml
│   │   │   ├── tech-writer.agent.yaml
│   │   │   ├── ux-designer.agent.yaml
│   │   │   └── mms-domain-expert.agent.yaml  # Custom for railway
│   │   ├── workflows/              # Workflow Definitions
│   │   │   ├── 1-analysis/
│   │   │   ├── 2-plan-workflows/
│   │   │   ├── 3-solutioning/
│   │   │   └── 4-implementation/
│   │   ├── data/                   # Templates and Checklists
│   │   └── docs/                   # Workflow Documentation
│   ├── cis/                        # Creative Intelligence Suite
│   ├── bmb/                        # BMad Builder
│   ├── custom/                     # MMS Custom Extensions
│   │   ├── agents/
│   │   │   ├── railway-domain-expert.agent.yaml
│   │   │   └── demo-coordinator.agent.yaml
│   │   └── workflows/
│   │       └── demo-validation/
│   └── project-context.md          # Project Configuration
│
├── docs/                           # Existing MMS Documentation
│   ├── README.md                   # Domain overview
│   ├── MMS_DEMO_PLAYBOOK.md        # Complete playbook
│   ├── MMS_PROFESSIONAL_PROFILES.md
│   ├── BMAD_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md # This file
│   └── RFQ.pdf
│
├── bmad-artifacts/                 # BMAD Generated Artifacts
│   ├── phase-1-analysis/
│   │   ├── research-findings.md
│   │   ├── competitive-analysis.md
│   │   └── product-brief.md
│   ├── phase-2-planning/
│   │   ├── prd.md                  # Product Requirements Document
│   │   ├── tech-spec.md            # Technical Specification
│   │   └── nfr.md                  # Non-Functional Requirements
│   ├── phase-3-solutioning/
│   │   ├── architecture.md
│   │   ├── ux-design-spec.md
│   │   ├── data-model.md
│   │   ├── epics/
│   │   │   ├── epic-01-dashboard.md
│   │   │   ├── epic-02-map.md
│   │   │   ├── epic-03-balancing.md
│   │   │   ├── epic-04-allocation.md
│   │   │   └── epic-05-simulation.md
│   │   └── stories/
│   └── phase-4-implementation/
│       ├── sprint-status.yaml
│       ├── sprint-01/
│       ├── sprint-02/
│       └── ...
│
├── bmad-knowledge/                 # Persistent Knowledge Base
│   ├── domain/
│   │   ├── railway-glossary.md
│   │   ├── maintenance-intervals.md
│   │   └── competitor-analysis.md
│   ├── decisions/
│   │   └── adr/                    # Architecture Decision Records
│   └── research/
│
└── src/                            # Application Source Code
    ├── frontend/
    ├── backend/
    └── solver/
```

---

## 4. Phase-by-Phase Plan

### 4.1 Phase 0: BMAD Installation & Configuration

**Duration:** Day 1-2
**Owner:** Tech Lead

#### Actions:

1. **Install BMAD Framework**
   ```bash
   cd /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/mms/MFC
   npx bmad-method@alpha install
   ```

2. **Configure Project Context**
   - Create `_bmad/project-context.md` with MMS-specific settings
   - Configure language settings (Portuguese for docs, English for code)
   - Set planning_artifacts path to `bmad-artifacts/`
   - Set implementation_artifacts path to `bmad-artifacts/phase-4-implementation/`

3. **Initialize Workflow Status**
   - Run `*workflow-init` with Analyst agent
   - Select "BMad Method" track (Level 2)
   - Set project_name: "MMS - Metro Mileage Management System"

4. **Create Custom Agents** (see Section 5)

#### Deliverables:
- [ ] `_bmad/` folder with all agents installed
- [ ] `_bmad/project-context.md` configured
- [ ] `bmm-workflow-status.yaml` initialized
- [ ] Custom railway domain agent created

---

### 4.2 Phase 1: Analysis

**Duration:** Sprint 1 (Weeks 1-2)
**BMAD Workflows:** Brainstorming, Research, Product Brief
**Agents:** Analyst, PM

Given the existing documentation, Phase 1 will focus on:

#### 4.2.1 Research Consolidation

**Workflow:** `*research` or `*deep-research`

| Task | Input | Output |
|------|-------|--------|
| Domain validation | MMS_DEMO_PLAYBOOK.md §2 | `bmad-knowledge/domain/` |
| Competitive analysis | MMS_DEMO_PLAYBOOK.md §3 | `competitive-analysis.md` |
| Unknown unknowns review | §2.4 | Validated domain knowledge |

#### 4.2.2 Product Brief Creation

**Workflow:** `*create-product-brief`

Extract from existing playbook:
- Vision and objectives (§1)
- Target users (§7.1 Personas)
- Success metrics (§1.3)
- Scope boundaries (§4.5, §4.6)

#### Deliverables:
- [ ] `bmad-artifacts/phase-1-analysis/product-brief.md`
- [ ] `bmad-artifacts/phase-1-analysis/research-findings.md`
- [ ] `bmad-knowledge/domain/railway-glossary.md` (from §2.1, Appendix A)
- [ ] Workflow status updated to Phase 2 ready

---

### 4.3 Phase 2: Planning (Required)

**Duration:** Sprint 1-2 (Weeks 1-4)
**BMAD Workflows:** PRD Creation, Tech Spec
**Agents:** PM, Architect

#### 4.3.1 PRD Creation

**Workflow:** `*prd`

Formalize requirements from MMS_DEMO_PLAYBOOK.md:

| PRD Section | Source |
|-------------|--------|
| Problem Statement | §1.1-1.2, §4.1 |
| User Personas | §7.1 |
| Functional Requirements | §5.1-5.2 |
| Non-Functional Requirements | §6 |
| Success Criteria | §1.3, §4.2 |
| Scope (In/Out) | §4.5-4.6 |
| Risks | §11 |

#### 4.3.2 Technical Specification

**Workflow:** `*create-tech-spec` (for Quick Flow components)

Focus areas:
- Solver integration (§8.4)
- API design (§8.5)
- Cache strategy (§8.6)
- gRPC communication (§8.7)

#### Deliverables:
- [ ] `bmad-artifacts/phase-2-planning/prd.md`
- [ ] `bmad-artifacts/phase-2-planning/tech-spec.md`
- [ ] `bmad-artifacts/phase-2-planning/nfr.md`
- [ ] Workflow status updated to Phase 3 ready

---

### 4.4 Phase 3: Solutioning (Required)

**Duration:** Sprint 2-3 (Weeks 3-6)
**BMAD Workflows:** Architecture, UX Design, Epic/Story Creation
**Agents:** Architect, UX Designer, PM, SM

#### 4.4.1 Architecture Design

**Workflow:** `*create-architecture`

Formalize from §8:
- System architecture diagram
- Component specifications
- Data model (§8.3)
- API contracts (§8.5)
- Integration patterns (§8.7)

#### 4.4.2 UX Design

**Workflow:** `*create-ux-spec`

Formalize from §7:
- User journeys (§7.2)
- Wireframes (§7.5)
- Component library (§7.3.2)
- Design system (colors, typography)

#### 4.4.3 Epic & Story Creation

**Workflow:** `*create-epics-and-stories`

Based on §5 and §10, create:

| Epic | Stories Est. | Priority |
|------|--------------|----------|
| E1: Dashboard & KPIs | 8-10 | Must Have |
| E2: Interactive Map | 6-8 | Must Have |
| E3: Mileage Balancing | 8-10 | Must Have |
| E4: Train Allocation | 10-12 | Must Have |
| E5: What-If Simulation | 8-10 | Must Have |
| E6: Gantt Maintenance | 6-8 | Should Have |
| E7: Alerts System | 4-6 | Should Have |
| E8: Seed Data & Demo | 4-6 | Must Have |

#### 4.4.4 Implementation Readiness Check

**Workflow:** `*check-implementation-readiness`

Validate:
- Architecture completeness
- Story acceptance criteria
- Technical dependencies resolved
- Team capacity aligned

#### Deliverables:
- [ ] `bmad-artifacts/phase-3-solutioning/architecture.md`
- [ ] `bmad-artifacts/phase-3-solutioning/ux-design-spec.md`
- [ ] `bmad-artifacts/phase-3-solutioning/data-model.md`
- [ ] `bmad-artifacts/phase-3-solutioning/epics/` (8 epic files)
- [ ] `bmad-artifacts/phase-3-solutioning/stories/` (50-60 story files)
- [ ] Implementation readiness checklist completed
- [ ] Workflow status updated to Phase 4 ready

---

### 4.5 Phase 4: Implementation (Iterative)

**Duration:** Sprints 3-6 (Weeks 5-12)
**BMAD Workflows:** Sprint Planning, Story Development, Code Review
**Agents:** SM, DEV, TEA, Tech Writer

#### 4.5.1 Sprint Structure

Following the 12-week timeline from §10:

| Sprint | Weeks | Focus | BMAD Workflow |
|--------|-------|-------|---------------|
| 3 | 5-6 | Core Solver + API Foundation | `*sprint-start`, `*work-next-story` |
| 4 | 7-8 | Frontend Base (Dashboard, Map) | `*work-next-story`, `*code-review` |
| 5 | 9-10 | Integration + Validation | `*work-next-story`, `*correct-course` |
| 6 | 11-12 | Demo Polish + Rehearsal | `*sprint-finish`, validation |

#### 4.5.2 Story Lifecycle

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                     BMAD STORY LIFECYCLE                            │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                      │
│   backlog ──▶ ready-for-dev ──▶ in-progress ──▶ review ──▶ done    │
│      │             │                │            │          │       │
│      ▼             ▼                ▼            ▼          ▼       │
│   SM assigns    DEV picks      DEV codes     TEA tests   SM closes │
│   priority      from queue     + unit test   + reviews   sprint    │
│                                                                      │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

#### 4.5.3 Key Workflows per Sprint

**Sprint Start:**
```
*sprint-start → SM agent
- Select stories for sprint
- Assign priorities
- Set sprint goals
```

**Story Development:**
```
*work-next-story → DEV agent
- Get next story from queue
- Receive just-in-time context
- Implement with TDD
```

**Code Review:**
```
*code-review → TEA agent
- Run automated tests
- Validate against acceptance criteria
- Flag issues for correction
```

**Sprint End:**
```
*sprint-finish → SM agent
- Update story statuses
- Generate sprint report
- Plan next sprint
```

#### Deliverables:
- [ ] `bmad-artifacts/phase-4-implementation/sprint-status.yaml` (continuously updated)
- [ ] Sprint folders with completed stories
- [ ] Working demo at end of each sprint
- [ ] Final demo ready by Week 12

---

## 5. Customized Agents for MMS

### 5.1 Standard Agents (from BMM)

| Agent | Role | MMS Usage |
|-------|------|-----------|
| **PM (John)** | Product Manager | PRD creation, story prioritization |
| **Analyst** | Research & Analysis | Domain research, competitive analysis |
| **Architect** | System Design | Architecture, data model, API design |
| **UX Designer** | User Experience | Wireframes, component design |
| **SM (Scrum Master)** | Sprint Management | Sprint planning, story assignment |
| **DEV** | Developer | Story implementation |
| **TEA** | Test Architect | Testing, quality assurance |
| **Tech Writer** | Documentation | API docs, user guides |

### 5.2 Custom Agents for MMS Domain

#### 5.2.1 Railway Domain Expert

```yaml
# _bmad/custom/agents/railway-domain-expert.agent.yaml
agent:
  metadata:
    id: "_bmad/custom/agents/railway-domain-expert"
    name: Maria
    title: Railway Operations Specialist
    icon: 🚂
    module: custom

  persona:
    role: Railway Fleet Management Expert
    identity: >
      Senior railway operations professional with 15+ years experience in
      rolling stock management, fleet maintenance, and operational planning.
      Expert in European rail standards (EN 15380, ECM regulations).
    communication_style: >
      "Technical but accessible. Uses precise railway terminology while
      ensuring non-specialists understand. Challenges assumptions with
      real-world operational constraints."
    principles: |
      - Validate all system features against real railway operations
      - Ensure terminology matches industry standards
      - Identify operational constraints that may impact system design
      - Reference MMS_DEMO_PLAYBOOK.md Section 2 for domain context
      - Validate seed data realism against industry norms

  expertise:
    - Rolling stock lifecycle management
    - Preventive maintenance scheduling (PM1, PM2, HM, Overhaul)
    - Wheelset and bogie maintenance constraints
    - Dead running optimization
    - Depot capacity planning
    - Crew scheduling integration
    - European rail regulations and homologation

  menu:
    - trigger: VD or validate-domain
      description: "[VD] Validate domain terminology and concepts"
    - trigger: VS or validate-seed
      description: "[VS] Validate seed data realism"
    - trigger: VC or validate-constraints
      description: "[VC] Validate operational constraints in solver"
```

#### 5.2.2 Demo Coordinator

```yaml
# _bmad/custom/agents/demo-coordinator.agent.yaml
agent:
  metadata:
    id: "_bmad/custom/agents/demo-coordinator"
    name: Carlos
    title: Demo Presentation Specialist
    icon: 🎯
    module: custom

  persona:
    role: Enterprise Demo Strategist
    identity: >
      Pre-sales and demo specialist with 10+ years experience in
      enterprise software presentations. Expert in creating "wow moments"
      and handling executive stakeholder objections.
    communication_style: >
      "High-energy, focused on impact. Every feature explained in terms
      of business value. Anticipates objections before they arise."
    principles: |
      - Every demo element must answer "why should the client care?"
      - Reference MMS_DEMO_PLAYBOOK.md Section 12 for demo script
      - Maintain FAQ responses for common questions
      - Ensure 15-minute demo fits timeline perfectly
      - Prepare fallback scenarios for technical issues

  menu:
    - trigger: DR or demo-review
      description: "[DR] Review demo readiness"
    - trigger: DF or demo-faq
      description: "[DF] Review and expand FAQ"
    - trigger: DS or demo-script
      description: "[DS] Refine demo presentation script"
```

---

## 6. Workflow Sequence

### 6.1 Complete Workflow Map

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    MMS BMAD WORKFLOW SEQUENCE                                │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                              │
│  WEEK 1-2: PHASE 1 (ANALYSIS) + PHASE 2 START                               │
│  ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── │
│                                                                              │
│  Day 1-2:   [SETUP]                                                          │
│             └── Install BMAD → Configure project-context → Init workflow     │
│                                                                              │
│  Day 3-5:   [ANALYST] *research                                              │
│             └── Consolidate domain knowledge from playbook                   │
│                                                                              │
│  Day 6-7:   [ANALYST] *create-product-brief                                  │
│             └── Extract vision, users, success metrics                       │
│                                                                              │
│  Day 8-10:  [PM] *prd                                                        │
│             └── Create formal PRD from playbook requirements                 │
│                                                                              │
│  WEEK 3-4: PHASE 2 COMPLETE + PHASE 3 START                                  │
│  ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── │
│                                                                              │
│  Day 11-13: [ARCHITECT] *create-architecture                                 │
│             └── Formalize system architecture from §8                        │
│                                                                              │
│  Day 14-16: [UX-DESIGNER] *create-ux-spec                                    │
│             └── Create design system, wireframes from §7                     │
│                                                                              │
│  Day 17-20: [PM] *create-epics-and-stories                                   │
│             └── Generate 8 epics, 50-60 stories                              │
│                                                                              │
│  WEEK 5-6: PHASE 3 COMPLETE + PHASE 4 START (SPRINT 3)                       │
│  ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── │
│                                                                              │
│  Day 21:    [ALL] *check-implementation-readiness                            │
│             └── Validate all artifacts complete                              │
│                                                                              │
│  Day 22:    [SM] *sprint-start                                               │
│             └── Sprint 3: Core Solver + API                                  │
│                                                                              │
│  Day 23-30: [DEV] *work-next-story (repeat)                                  │
│             └── Implement solver and API foundation                          │
│                                                                              │
│  WEEK 7-8: SPRINT 4 (FRONTEND)                                               │
│  ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── │
│                                                                              │
│  Day 31:    [SM] *sprint-start                                               │
│             └── Sprint 4: Dashboard + Map + UI                               │
│                                                                              │
│  Day 32-44: [DEV] *work-next-story + [TEA] *code-review                      │
│             └── Build frontend components                                    │
│                                                                              │
│  WEEK 9-10: SPRINT 5 (INTEGRATION)                                           │
│  ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── │
│                                                                              │
│  Day 45:    [SM] *sprint-start                                               │
│             └── Sprint 5: Integration + Validation                           │
│                                                                              │
│  Day 46-50: [DEV] Integration work                                           │
│             └── Connect frontend ↔ API ↔ Solver                              │
│                                                                              │
│  Day 51-55: [RAILWAY-EXPERT] Validate domain accuracy                        │

│                                                                              │

└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

### 6.2 Agent-Workflow Matrix

| Phase | Primary Agent | Workflows | Output Artifacts |
|-------|---------------|-----------|------------------|
| 0 | Tech Lead | Install, Configure | `_bmad/`, `project-context.md` |
| 1 | Analyst | `*research`, `*product-brief` | Research findings, Product brief |
| 2 | PM | `*prd`, `*tech-spec` | PRD, Technical specification |
| 3 | Architect | `*create-architecture` | Architecture document |
| 3 | UX Designer | `*create-ux-spec` | UX specification, wireframes |
| 3 | PM + SM | `*create-epics-and-stories` | Epics, Stories, Backlog |
| 3 | All | `*check-implementation-readiness` | Readiness checklist |
| 4 | SM | `*sprint-start`, `*sprint-finish` | Sprint plans, reports |
| 4 | DEV | `*work-next-story` | Implemented features |
| 4 | TEA | `*code-review` | Test results, quality reports |
| 4 | Railway Expert | Domain validation | Validated terminology, data |
| 4 | Demo Coordinator | Demo prep | Script, FAQ, fallbacks |

---

## 7. Sprint Plan Integration

### 7.1 Mapping Playbook Sprints to BMAD

The MMS_DEMO_PLAYBOOK.md §10 defines 6 sprints. Here's how they map to BMAD phases:

| Playbook Sprint | BMAD Phase | BMAD Sprints | Focus |
|-----------------|------------|--------------|-------|
| Sprint 1 (Wk 1-2) | Phase 1+2 | Pre-implementation | Setup, Analysis, PRD |
| Sprint 2 (Wk 3-4) | Phase 2+3 | Pre-implementation | Planning, Architecture |
| Sprint 3 (Wk 5-6) | Phase 4 | BMAD Sprint 1 | Core Solver + API |
| Sprint 4 (Wk 7-8) | Phase 4 | BMAD Sprint 2 | Frontend Base |
| Sprint 5 (Wk 9-10) | Phase 4 | BMAD Sprint 3 | Integration |
| Sprint 6 (Wk 11-12) | Phase 4 | BMAD Sprint 4 | Demo Prep |

### 7.2 Story Distribution by Sprint

Based on §5 and §10:

**BMAD Sprint 1 (Weeks 5-6): Solver + API**
- Stories: 12-15
- Epics: E4 (Allocation core), Backend infrastructure
- Agents: DEV (Python/OR-Tools), Architect

**BMAD Sprint 2 (Weeks 7-8): Frontend**
- Stories: 15-18
- Epics: E1 (Dashboard), E2 (Map), E3 (Balancing viz)
- Agents: DEV (Frontend), UX Designer

**BMAD Sprint 3 (Weeks 9-10): Integration**
- Stories: 10-12
- Epics: E4 (complete), E5 (Simulation), E7 (Alerts)
- Agents: DEV (Full-stack), TEA, Railway Expert

**BMAD Sprint 4 (Weeks 11-12): Polish**
- Stories: 8-10
- Epics: E6 (Gantt if time), E8 (Seed Data), Demo polish
- Agents: All + Demo Coordinator

---

## 8. Folder Structure

### 8.1 Creation Script

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# create-bmad-structure.sh

# Base directories
mkdir -p _bmad/{bmm,cis,bmb,custom}/{agents,workflows}
mkdir -p bmad-artifacts/{phase-1-analysis,phase-2-planning,phase-3-solutioning,phase-4-implementation}
mkdir -p bmad-artifacts/phase-3-solutioning/{epics,stories}
mkdir -p bmad-artifacts/phase-4-implementation/{sprint-01,sprint-02,sprint-03,sprint-04}
mkdir -p bmad-knowledge/{domain,decisions/adr,research}
mkdir -p src/{frontend,backend,solver}

# Create placeholder files
touch bmad-artifacts/phase-4-implementation/sprint-status.yaml
touch _bmad/project-context.md

echo "BMAD folder structure created successfully!"
```

### 8.2 Key Files Description

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `_bmad/project-context.md` | Project configuration, variables, settings |
| `_bmad/bmm-workflow-status.yaml` | Current workflow phase tracking |
| `bmad-artifacts/phase-2-planning/prd.md` | Product Requirements Document |
| `bmad-artifacts/phase-3-solutioning/architecture.md` | System Architecture |
| `bmad-artifacts/phase-4-implementation/sprint-status.yaml` | Sprint tracking |
| `bmad-knowledge/domain/railway-glossary.md` | Domain terminology |

---

## 9. Installation Steps

### 9.1 Prerequisites

- Node.js 18+ installed
- IDE with BMAD support (Claude Code, Cursor, VS Code)
- Git repository initialized

### 9.2 Step-by-Step Installation

```bash
# Step 1: Navigate to project root
cd /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/mms/MFC

# Step 2: Install BMAD V6 Alpha
npx bmad-method@alpha install

# Step 3: Select modules during installation
# - [x] BMad Method (BMM) - Required
# - [x] Creative Intelligence Suite (CIS) - Recommended
# - [x] BMad Builder (BMB) - For custom agents

# Step 4: Configure project settings
# - Project name: MMS - Metro Mileage Management System
# - Skill level: Expert
# - Planning artifacts: bmad-artifacts/
# - Implementation artifacts: bmad-artifacts/phase-4-implementation/
# - Knowledge folder: bmad-knowledge/

# Step 5: Create custom agents
# Copy custom agent YAML files to _bmad/custom/agents/

# Step 6: Initialize workflow
# Load Analyst agent in IDE, then run:
# *workflow-init
```

### 9.3 Post-Installation Verification

```bash
# Verify installation
ls -la _bmad/

# Expected output:
# _bmad/
# ├── bmm/
# ├── cis/
# ├── bmb/
# ├── custom/
# └── project-context.md

# Verify workflow status
cat _bmad/bmm-workflow-status.yaml
```

---

## 10. Success Metrics

### 10.1 Phase Completion Criteria

| Phase | Success Criteria | Verification |
|-------|------------------|--------------|
| Phase 0 | BMAD installed, project-context configured | `_bmad/` exists, status file created |
| Phase 1 | Research consolidated, product brief complete | Artifacts in phase-1 folder |
| Phase 2 | PRD approved, tech spec complete | PRD reviewed by PM agent |
| Phase 3 | Architecture validated, stories created | Readiness check passes |
| Phase 4 | Demo functional, all Must-Have stories done | Demo runs 15 min without crash |

### 10.2 Key Performance Indicators

| Metric | Target | Measurement |
|--------|--------|-------------|
| Story velocity | 8-10 stories/sprint | Sprint status tracking |
| Test coverage | >80% for core modules | TEA agent reports |
| Domain accuracy | 100% correct terminology | Railway Expert validation |
| Demo stability | 3 runs without bugs | Rehearsal tracking |
| Client impression | "They understand our problem" | Demo feedback |

### 10.3 Risk Mitigation via BMAD

| Risk (from §11) | BMAD Mitigation |
|-----------------|-----------------|
| R1: Parecer genérico | Railway Domain Expert agent validates all terminology |
| R2: Solver lento | TEA agent performance testing throughout |
| R3: Perguntas sem resposta | Demo Coordinator maintains comprehensive FAQ |
| R4: Bug durante demo | Continuous *code-review, rehearsal tracking |
| R5: Escopo creep | PM agent guards backlog, SM manages sprint scope |
| R6: Equipe sem conhecimento | Phase 1 analysis consolidates domain knowledge |

---
